name: Thai Carrot Soup
recipeInstructions:
1. Medium-sized onions thinly sliced.
2. Trimmed, scrubbed, and thinly sliced carrots.
3. Skim fat from chicken stock or broth.
4. Heat oil in a 3-quart saucepan over medium-high heat.
5. Add onion, garlic, ginger, and carrots to the pan. Cook, stirring frequently, until hot and fragrant – about 4 minutes.
6. Add 3 cups of chicken stock or broth. Simmer, covered, until carrots are tender – about 25 minutes.
7. Drain liquid from solids. Reserve liquid. Puree solids with cilantro in a blender or processor until smooth. Add as much liquid as the container can hold. Continue pureeing until evenly smooth.
8. Transfer to a 3-quart bowl. Add remaining liquid, if any, and remaining 1 cup of stock. Season to taste with salt and crushed red pepper. Serve chilled or hot.
NOTE: This dish can be made up to 2 days in advance and refrigerated, or frozen for up to 3 months. Adjust seasonings before serving. Each serving contains about: 115 calories, 614 mg sodium, 1 mg cholesterol, 4 g fat, 17 g carbohydrates, 5 g protein, 1.55 g fiber.
